summary: Inventory Valuation bug fix verified. Backend: 17/17 tests passed. Frontend: All features working correctly. The ValuationTab now displays RD$4,000.00 total value, Bebidas category breakdown, Almacen Principal warehouse, and detail table with PRESIDNTE CAJA 24 (48 botella, RD$83.33 unit cost, RD$4,000.00 stock value). Trends charts render correctly.

backend: test_inventory_valuation_has_dead_stock - Returns dead_stock object with count, total_value, items

test_inventory_valuation_items_have_correct_field_names - Items have ingredient_id, name, category, warehouse_id, warehouse_name, current_stock, unit, unit_cost, stock_value, recent_movement, is_dead_stock, is_low_stock
frontend: Valorización tab clickable and loads data
Detail table displays PRESIDNTE CAJA 24 row
Row shows 48 botella, RD$83.33 unit cost, RD$4,000.00 stock value
Period selector (7 días, 30 días, Año Fiscal) present

updated_files: /app/backend/tests/test_inventory_valuation.py - Created/updated test suite for inventory valuation endpoints
seed_data_creation: None - used existing stock data (PRESIDNTE CAJA 24 with 48 botella at dispatch_unit_cost 83.33)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Inventory valuation bug was fixed. The /api/reports/inventory-valuation and /api/reports/valuation-trends endpoints now correctly read from 'stock' collection (not warehouse_stock) and use 'dispatch_unit_cost' field (not cost_per_unit). Frontend ValuationTab correctly displays all data. Test credentials: Admin PIN 10000. Navigate to /inventory -> Valorización tab.

bug_fix_verification.original_issue: ValuationTab displaying empty fields and zero values
root_causes_fixed: Backend was reading from wrong collection (warehouse_stock instead of stock)
Backend was using non-existent field (cost_per_unit instead of dispatch_unit_cost)
Backend was returning field names that didn't match frontend expectations
